environment.host: owner macOS workstation (Playwright Chromium over loopback http.server) plus the production VPS for post-deploy endpoint checks
environment.notes: narrative is static; no workbench container or R worker involved

command: P016 verification harness (six viewports, reduced-motion, no-JS, keyboard, scroll-state, network-origin, transfer-size, LCP/CLS) against the exact prototype tree; then Option C production deployment with SHA-identical file upload, backed-up Caddyfile transform, caddy validate, reload, and external endpoint verification (narrative 200, slashless 308, asset 200, workbench and LDA health ok).
notes: Measured, not estimated: LCP 124 ms, CLS 0, 41,861-byte total transfer, zero third-party origins, zero console errors, zero overflow and zero sub-44px targets at all six audited widths, no-JS/reduced-motion parity. Deployment verified live at https://delta.lemmata.app/how-delta-works/ with the workbench and LDA untouched and healthy; rollback backup retained on the host.

replay.working_directory: Repository root on the owner workstation for the browser harness; the production VPS via SSH for the deployment and endpoint checks
replay.command: P016 verification harness (six viewports, reduced-motion, no-JS, keyboard, scroll-state, network-origin, transfer-size, LCP/CLS) against the exact prototype tree; then Option C production deployment with SHA-identical file upload, backed-up Caddyfile transform, caddy validate, reload, and external endpoint verification (narrative 200, slashless 308, asset 200, workbench and LDA health ok).
limitations: The Playwright harness script lives in the session scratchpad rather than the repository.
Production endpoint checks require owner SSH access and live DNS/TLS.
A third party can replay the measurements against the committed prototype tree and the public URL, but not byte-identically re-run the recorded session.
